LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT AVANDE, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. MATTER CALLED. MS. LEE INFORMS THE COURT THAT THE WRONG LINK WAS ACCESSED WHEN CHECKING FOR THE TENTATIVE RULING. THE COURT STATES THAT OPPOSING COUNSEL IS NOT PRESENT AND ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT AVANDE, INC.'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFFS' FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. THE COURT GRANTS DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE. (SEE EVID. CODE SEC. 452(D) [RECORDS OF COURT].) DEFENDANT MAY RENEW THE CLAIM PRECLUSION ISSUE BASED ON APPLICABLE DELAWARE LAW THROUGH A PROPERLY NOTICED SUBSEQUENT FILING. ORDER SIGNED. (SEE MINI-MINUTES OR ORDER FILED THIS DATE FOR THE COURT'S COMPLETE RULING) JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA; COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR NO. 8600, EMAIL: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM. (302/EPS)

calendar.matters:
Judgment Debtor Avande, Inc.'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Acknowledgement Of Satisfaction Of Judgment; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; And Declarations Of Gregg A. Martin, Esq. And Ann S. Lee, Esq. (Motions)
Off Calendar Apr-28-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Stephen McDonagh, Esq. (415.753.2959; smcdonagh@vbjusa.com) for Plaintiffs.
Ann Lee, Esq. (310.552.9292; alee@hkemlaw.com) of Hamburg, Karic, Edwards & Martin LLP for Defendant.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Judgment Debtor Avande, Inc.'s Motion To Compel Acknowledgement Of Satisfaction Of Judgment.

Defendant Avande, Inc.'s motion to compel acknowledgment of satisfaction is denied.
Defendant relies on Wade v. Schrader (2008)168 Cal.App.4th 1039, for the proposition that "a motion to compel acknowledgment of satisfaction of a judgment... is an entirely acceptable procedure for balancing an offsetting judgment." (Id. at 1048-49.) However, Wade makes it clear that an entry of an offset is within the equitable discretion of this Court. (Wade, 168 Cal.App.4th at 1048 ["[T]he superior court's decision to apply the [offset] credit in partial satisfaction of the judgment was an exercise of the court's equitable discretion."].) As the Court explained in its order to release funds to Plaintiffs, the Court is not persuaded that an offset is proper here. The Court is thus not inclined to exercise its equitable authority in their favor.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

Motion For Order To Release Funds To Plaintiffs
Off Calendar Apr-28-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Stephen McDonagh, Esq. (415.753.2959; smcdonagh@vbjusa.com) for Plaintiffs.
Ann Lee, Esq. (310.552.9292; alee@hkemlaw.com) of Hamburg, Karic, Edwards & Martin LLP for Defendant.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFFS SHAWN EVANS and JUDITH EVANS' Motion For Order To Release Funds To Plaintiffs.

Plaintiffs Shawn Evans and Judith Evans's motion for an order to release funds is granted. The funds being held by the San Francisco Sheriff in the approximate amount of $61,319.30 shall be released and paid to the Plaintiffs.

Defendant Avande, Inc.'s opposition fails for two separate reasons. First, Defendant contends the writ of attachment has expired under CCP section 488.510 which states, "(a) Unless sooner released or discharged, any attachment shall cease to be of any force or effect, and the property levied upon shall be released from the operation of the attachment, at the expiration of three years from the date of issuance of the writ of attachment under which the levy was made." (CCP 488.510(b).) However, pursuant to this Court's June 7, 2018, order, "The instant action is stayed in its entirety pending the completion of the Delaware case or further order of this Court." The stay order was then lifted on January 13, 2021. Therefore, the writ will not expire until May 2023. (CCP 404.5(c) ["Unless otherwise specified in the stay order, a stay order suspends all proceedings in the action to which it applies."].)

Second, Defendant contends that on January 27, 2022, Defendant notified Plaintiffs it was offsetting the Delaware Judgments in the then-owed amount of $95,327.01 against the Judgment at issue and would pay the owed balance of $54,672.99 by check. This Court previously determined that "defendant fails to show that plaintiff Judith Evans was in privity with co-plaintiff Shawn Evans such that the application of claim preclusion is warranted. Second, the same claims/issues pertaining to the promissory notes were not decided in Delaware." (July 2021 Order.) Similarly, the judgments in the Delaware actions cannot apply to the Judgment at issue here. Defendants fail to cite any authority establishing their right to offset judgment. In fact, it is a well-settled proposition that where there exist judgments between the same parties of the same general character the one may be offset against the other. (California Cotton Credit Corp. v. Superior Court of Madera County (1932) 127 Cal. App. 472, 474.) Here, Plaintiff Judith Evans was not a party to any of the Delaware actions. Moreover, under CCP section 998, "[t]he written offer shall include a statement of the offer, containing the terms and conditions of the judgment or award, and a provision that allows the accepting party to indicate acceptance of the offer by signing a statement that the offer is accepted. Any acceptance of the offer, whether made on the document containing the offer or on a separate document of acceptance, shall be in writing and shall be signed by counsel for the accepting party or, if not represented by counsel, by the accepting party. (CCP 998.) In this case, nothing in the settlement offer made reference to Defendant's intention to offset the Delaware judgments. Finally, Defendant does not present any evidence that the settlement amount was paid.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-16-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances (via ZOOM videoconference):
Atty. Ann Lee, Esq. of Hamburg Karic Edwards & Martin LLP, 310.552.9292, for Defendant Avande, Inc.;
No other appearance by any party or counsel.

DEFENDANT AVANDE, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Matter called. Ms. Lee informs the Court that the wrong link was accessed when checking for the tentative ruling.

The Court states that opposing counsel is not present and adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant Avande, Inc.'s demurrer to plaintiffs' first amended complaint is overruled.

The Court grants defendant's request for judicial notice. (See Evid. Code sec. 452(d) [records of court].)

Defendant's moving papers rely on California law to argue that the Delaware judgment in Avande, Inc. v. Shawn Evans, et al., CA No. 2018-0203-AGB (Del. Ct. Ch.) bars the instant action based on the principle of res judicata (claim preclusion). Plaintiffs aptly note, however, that Delaware law, not California law, governs the preclusive effect of the Delaware judgment. "Under California law, both the validity and effect of a foreign judgment are governed by the laws of the state in which it is rendered. (Krofcheck v. Ensign Co. (1980) 112 Cal.App.3d 558, 169 Cal.Rptr. 516.)" (Brinker v. Superior Court (1991) 235 Cal.App.3d 1296, 1300.) Therefore, defendant's moving papers rely on inapposite legal authority and it may not properly re-mint its demurrer in its reply brief. (See American Indian Model Schools v. Oakland Unified School Dist. (2014) 227 Cal.App.4th 258, 276 ["Fairness militates against allowing [a party] to raise an issue for the first time in a reply brief because consideration of the issue deprives the respondent of the opportunity to counter the appellant by raising opposing arguments about the new issue."].) Likewise, defendant's supplemental request for judicial notice belatedly filed with its reply papers is denied. Defendant may renew the claim preclusion issue based on applicable Delaware law through a properly noticed subsequent filing.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com. (302/EPS)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-16-2018 Continued To Feb-06-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay Or Dismiss Proceedings Pending Resolution Of Delaware Case; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Jun-07-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. Stephen B. McDonagh of Glasser & McDonagh, Tel. No. 1-415-753-2959 appearing for the Plaintiffs;
Atty. GreggA. Martin of hamburg Karic & Martin LLP, Tel. No. 1-310-552-9292
appearng for the Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant Avande, Inc.'s motion to stay proceedings pending resolution of the action pending in the Court of Chancery in the state of Delaware is granted. Per this court's authority to stay a later filed action in favor of a related previously filed action, this case is stayed pending the completion of the Delaware case or further order of this court. Judicial economy is disserved by the concurrent prosecution of two lawsuits involving the same parties seeking damages against each other in two different fora approximately 3000 miles apart. There is no persuasive reason why the claims alleged by plaintiffs Shawn and Judith Evans in this case should not be re-filed and prosecuted as cross-claims in the Delaware lawsuit. Since both the amended complaint in the Delaware lawsuit and the first amended complaint filed in this court seek monetary relief, the ultimate damages issues are who, as between Mr. Evans and Avande, owes money to the other and how much. Instead of resolving these issues piecemeal with the possibility of inconsistent rulings, the far better course is to have them resolved in a single case. Since this court has no authority to dismiss or stay the Delaware lawsuit, the only way this court can avoid the unnecessary expense and complications of two different lawsuits is to stay or dismiss this case. A stay is preferable to a dismissal so that, in the event that the Delaware court no longer is able or willing to adjudicate the parties' disputes, this case can be revived without the need for the filing of a new case. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR # 8600 =302/h
